administering_body: Los Angeles Department of Cultural Affairs (DCA)
notes: Developers may commission qualifying artwork on-site (meeting DCA's artwork standards) OR pay 1% of construction cost into the city's Cultural Development Fund as an in-lieu fee. The program applies to private development requiring a building permit in the City of Los Angeles. Art must be permanent and publicly accessible.
practical_notes: Historically underutilized by developers. Most satisfy the requirement through in-lieu fees rather than commissioned art. Not a reliable commissioning signal.

administering_body: Los Angeles Department of Cultural Affairs (DCA)
notes: 1% of the construction cost of public capital improvement projects is allocated to public art. Administered by DCA in coordination with the relevant city department. Applies to projects funded by the City of Los Angeles capital budget.
practical_notes: Public Works art program actively drives commissioning for city-funded capital projects.

ordinance_name: NYC Public Art Allocation for Public Capital Projects
notes: New York City DCLA administers a 1% public art allocation for eligible city-funded capital construction. The program is limited to public sector projects and requires owner/applicant identification as a city agency or public authority.
practical_notes: NYC DCLA percent-for-art actively drives commissioning for city capital projects.

notes: 2% of the construction cost of eligible city construction projects is allocated to public art. Administered by SFAC. Applies to projects funded by the City and County of San Francisco.
practical_notes: The highest percent-for-art rate in the US. 55-year track record. Administered by SFAC. Strong commissioning signal for public capital projects.

notes: Applies to private development projects in C-3 and approximately 15 other downtown/commercial zoning districts. Developers may commission artwork or pay in-lieu fee.
practical_notes: Has generated approximately 53 commissions in 30 years despite covering hundreds of qualifying projects. Trust fund option is largely dormant. Treat as legal context, not commissioning signal.
